note_evidence: 5–6 day history of symptoms beginning shortly after the holiday. Illness started with a dry cough
rationale: Dry cough onset 5-6 days ago after holiday documented.

note_evidence: followed by fever that has persisted
rationale: Fever after cough onset, persistent course documented.

note_evidence: worsening dyspnea. On the morning of presentation, she was unable to walk from bed to the bathroom without stopping twice to catch her breath
rationale: Progressive dyspnea with morning event documented.

note_evidence: chest tightness ("like the air is thin") but denies chest pain
seemed "a little foggy" this morning, but the patient remains alert and interactive
positive at urgent care and repeated positive in the ED (diagnosis confirmed)
denies chest pain or pressure, and states this is distinct from her prior heart attack
rationale: Denial of chest pain with prior MI comparison documented.
Denial of hemoptysis and leg swelling documented.
Denial of falls and fainting documented.
Tree nut allergy with swelling and flagging documented.

note_evidence: Prior ST-elevation myocardial infarction (STEMI), years ago
Chest X-ray: findings consistent with pneumonia (viral inflammation of the lungs)
rationale: Pneumonia with viral inflammation on CXR documented.

note_evidence: hypoxemic respiratory failure requiring supplemental oxygen
dropped to ~76% while supine ... trending upward toward 39°C ... increased to high 80s
rationale: Supine desaturation, fever and HR rise documented.
Leukopenia noted but numeric value not given.
Thrombocytopenia noted but value not given.

note_evidence: Creatinine: elevated at 2.7 with markedly reduced estimated GFR

rationale: eGFR reduction noted but value ~11 not stated.
Mild hyperkalemia noted but value 5.07 not given.

note_evidence: Chest X-ray: findings consistent with pneumonia
Continuous supplemental oxygen via mask, titrated to maintain safe saturations
rationale: Continuous oxygen by mask titrated documented.

note_evidence: Prone positioning sessions daily, as tolerated, with attention to spinal/hip comfort given scoliosis (padding ... scheduled rotation)
rationale: Daily prone positioning with scoliosis padding documented.

note_evidence: Daily morning labs: CBC, chemistry panel ... troponin ... coagulation studies ... iron studies
rationale: Daily labs including all listed tests documented.

note_evidence: Cautious/deliberate management of antihypertensive medications while kidneys recover, adjusted based on daily labs
rationale: Adjusting antihypertensives pending renal recovery documented.

note_evidence: Patient instructed to use call button immediately for worsening dyspnea, sensation that oxygen is inadequate, chest pain, or palpitations
high risk for severe disease ... One-day-at-a-time approach discussed with patient and daughter
severe infection poses risk for cardiac events ... Continue aspirin and cardiac medications as labs allow. Serial troponin monitoring.
rationale: Cardiac risk with troponin monitoring and continued aspirin documented.

note_evidence: Visitors not permitted in room; daily video calls ... Physician to call daughter (Teresa) each evening with updates
rationale: Isolation precautions with video calls and evening call documented.

note_evidence: Tree nut allergy flagged to dietary services
History obtained with assistance from daughter (Teresa) at bedside.
rationale: Daughter Teresa involved in history documented.

note_evidence: After application of oxygen mask, SpO2 improved to low 90s.
rationale: SpO2 improved to low 90s on mask documented.
